_comment: Connector registry (M11b): the single source of truth for the env-bearer connectors, read by BOTH proxy_addon.py (egress auth injection) and deploy.sh (--set-secrets wiring). Adding a connector = one entry here + a Secret Manager secret; NO code edit in either file. Each connector: domain (host the proxy matches, plus its subdomains), secret (Secret Manager secret name deploy.sh wires as <env>=<secret>:latest), env (env var the proxy reads the credential from), header (Authorization template; {} is replaced by the credential). The two auth-SHAPED exceptions, gcp (metadata-server SA token) and github (Basic base64 of the PAT), are NOT env-bearer and stay special-cased in code (proxy_addon.py) + always-on for github in deploy.sh; they are intentionally absent here. The entries below (resend, stripe, cloudflare, slack) are EXAMPLES that show the shape -- keep, remove, or replace them with whatever APIs your loops call; none activate unless a loop declares the connector AND the secret exists.
_env_only_note: A connector with NO 'domain' is wired as an env var by deploy.sh but ignored by the proxy (proxy_addon.py only injects when 'domain' AND 'env' are set). Use this shape for a credential a loop's agent consumes directly (e.g. types into a browser form / injects into a browser session) rather than one the proxy injects into an Authorization header. Loop-specific connectors live in that loop's OWN loops/<name>/connectors.json (merged over this generic registry at deploy time), so this engine repo stays task-agnostic.
